name: MSM
summary: MSM is a sulfur-containing compound with limited-to-moderate evidence for modest osteoarthritis pain reduction.
description: MSM is a sulfur-containing compound with limited-to-moderate evidence for modest osteoarthritis pain reduction.
safetyNotes: GI upset, headache, and insomnia possible.

claimMap.claim: v9.7: MSM has small RCT evidence for knee OA symptoms; keep below stronger joint options.
claimMap.notes: v9.7: MSM has small RCT evidence for knee OA symptoms; keep below stronger joint options.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: 3.375 g/day in knee OA RCT; 6 g/day evaluated for musculoskeletal pain safety

safety: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: PubMed PMID 21708034. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.citation: PubMed PMID 21708034. Minimal citation row added from existing workbook PMID; title/year/journal still require PubMed metadata extraction.
sources.note: evidence citation lookup; PMID source present in Evidence_Register

contraindications: Anticoagulant or antiplatelet medications (theoretical additive bleeding risk from platelet effects)
scheduled surgery (discontinue beforehand due to theoretical bleeding risk)
high doses (dose-dependent GI upset such as bloating or diarrhea or nausea)
